memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but the term project due Thursday is barely started. The easy chore is tempting, yet my achievement drive pushes me to prioritize the looming deadline over this quick win.
My afternoon is vanishing while the project remains untouched, and my low energy makes this procrastination even more frustrating.
My energy is critically low at 3.6, yet my achievement drive forces me to ignore the temptation of an easy chore in favor of this stalled project.
My energy is critically low, yet my achievement drive forces me to ignore the temptation of an easy chore in favor of this stalled project.

desires: Finish the term project draft before my energy completely crashes, so I don't have to beg for an extension from Prof.
Avoid looking like a slacker to Cohort_3 by proving I can prioritize the high-stakes work over their easy hangout plans.
Get this stupid trivial chore done immediately just to silence my brain's nagging guilt about unfinished tasks.

goals.description: Finish the term project draft immediately. Stop the trivial chore, it's a distraction from the real stakes.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive demands I crush the high-value academic task before my energy crashes. Procrastinating on this makes me look incompetent to Prof and wastes time I could be using to prove my worth.

agent.last_reasoning: I'll crush this trivial chore first; it's an instant win that clears my head for the term project. Ignoring it feels like wasting time, and finishing now lets me focus entirely on the high-stakes draft without distraction.

goals.description: Open the term project file and write exactly 300 words of core analysis immediately to break the paralysis.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive is screaming that a 'soft' deadline due in 3 days with 0% real progress is an unacceptable risk. I need tangible output NOW to silence the procrastination bias, even if it's just getting started. The 'easy chore' is a dopamine trap; doing it now will only let me off the hook for the big task.

goals.description: Cancel or skip the planned meal with cohort_3 until after Thursday.
goals.rationale: I value my reputation as a high-performer above social cohesion right now. Missing this meal hurts relationships (weight 0.15), but failing the project hurts my identity (weight 0.5). I'll assertively tell them I'm swamped later rather than wasting energy on small talk now.

desires: Crush this term project before the easy chore distracts me, because missing a deadline feels like a personal failure I can't afford.
Get something tangible done in the next hour to silence the nagging voice that I'm procrastinating on the big task.
Maintain my reputation as someone who gets things done with the cohort, even if it means skipping the meal they want to eat together.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: I'm ignoring the easy chore trap to hit the term project. My achievement drive demands tangible output now, not instant gratification. Skipping today's meal with cohort_3 is necessary; my identity as a high-performer outweighs their feelings right now.

trajectory.observation: Worked on Term project (due in 3 days) for 2.0h; progress=1.00.

goals.description: Draft a blunt, direct message to teammate_A asserting that I need their input only after I've completed the core draft by Thursday noon.
goals.rationale: I can't afford to waste mental energy negotiating social niceties with teammates while I'm behind on the main deliverable. My assertiveness style means I'll set this boundary directly once I have some leverage from making actual progress.

desires: Force myself to stop this trivial chore immediately so I can actually make progress on the term project before Thursday.
Regain enough mental focus to finish a substantial chunk of the assignment in the next two hours instead of wasting more time.
Avoid looking incompetent or unreliable to my team when we inevitably have to present this half-finished work.
